skill.name: firecrawl-download
skill.description: Download an entire website as local files - markdown, screenshots, or multiple formats per page. Use this skill when the user wants to save a site locally, download documentation for offline use, bulk-save pages as files, or says 'download the site', 'save as local files', 'offline copy', 'download all the docs', or 'save for reference'. Combines site mapping and scraping into organized local directories.
skill.summary: Download complete websites as organized local file directories with markdown, screenshots, or multiple formats per page.
skill.author: firecrawl

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 34 patterns across external_commands (23), network (6), and filesystem (3) categories. All findings evaluated as FALSE POSITIVES: external_commands are documented CLI usage with allowed-tools declaration, network URLs are documentation examples (docs.example.com), and filesystem references are markdown relative links to other skill files. No malicious intent detected. Minor risk from legitimate Bash command execution and network access for web scraping.

low_findings.title: External Command Execution via CLI
low_findings.description: Skill executes firecrawl CLI commands via Bash. Commands are documented examples with user-provided URLs. Risk is mitigated by allowed-tools declaration restricting to firecrawl and npx firecrawl commands only.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Allowed-tools declaration explicitly limits Bash to firecrawl commands - legitimate CLI tooling pattern with bounded execution scope.

low_findings.title: Network Access for Web Scraping
low_findings.description: Skill performs network requests to user-specified URLs for web scraping. This is expected behavior for a download tool but requires user trust in target URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network access is core functionality for web scraping - legitimate use case but requires user awareness of target URL trustworthiness.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Write Access
low_findings.description: Skill writes scraped content to local .firecrawl/ directory. Creates nested directory structure for downloaded pages.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Filesystem writes are expected for download functionality - content saved to dedicated .firecrawl directory with predictable structure.

content.value_statement: Save complete websites for offline access without manual page-by-page downloading. Firecrawl-download automates site mapping and content extraction into organized local directories.
actual_capabilities: Discovers all pages on a website using site mapping
Downloads pages as markdown, HTML, or multiple formats simultaneously
Captures screenshots of pages including full-page options
Filters content by URL paths, subdomains, or search queries
Organizes downloaded content into nested directory structure
Excludes non-main content like navigation and footers
limitations: Requires firecrawl CLI to be installed and configured with API key
Cannot download pages behind authentication without credentials
Rate limits and quotas apply based on Firecrawl service tier
Dynamic JavaScript-rendered content may require wait-for option

use_cases.title: Documentation Archive
use_cases.description: Download complete software documentation sites for offline reference during travel or low-connectivity environments.
use_cases.target_user: Developer traveling without reliable internet access

use_cases.title: Competitive Research
use_cases.description: Save competitor product pages and feature documentation for analysis and comparison without repeated site visits.

use_cases.title: Content Migration Backup
use_cases.description: Create local backups of existing website content before migrating to new platforms or redesigning.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Website Download
prompt_templates.prompt: Download all pages from https://docs.example.com as markdown files to my local machine.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Simple download of entire documentation site

prompt_templates.title: Download with Screenshots
prompt_templates.prompt: Download the Firecrawl docs at https://docs.firecrawl.dev with screenshots of each page, limit to 50 pages.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - Adding visual captures alongside text content

prompt_templates.title: Filtered Section Download
prompt_templates.prompt: Download only the SDK documentation from https://api.example.com/docs - include paths containing /sdks and /api, exclude all language translations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - Targeted download with path filters

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Format Archive with Filters
prompt_templates.prompt: Download https://knowledge.company.com including markdown, links, and full-page screenshots. Include only /features and /integration paths, exclude /internal and /draft. Wait for .content-loaded class before capturing.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced - Complete production workflow with all options

best_practices: Always use the -y flag in automated workflows to skip interactive confirmation prompts
Set reasonable page limits with --limit to avoid unexpected quota consumption
Use --exclude-paths to filter out non-essential content like translations, drafts, or internal pages
Test with a small limit first to verify configuration before large downloads
anti_patterns: Downloading entire large sites without setting a page limit first
Using full-page screenshots for every page when thumbnail views would suffice
Running downloads without the -y flag in automated scripts causing hangs
Including sensitive internal URLs that should not be cached locally

faq.question: What is the difference between firecrawl-download and firecrawl-crawl?
faq.answer: Download saves pages as local files in organized directories (markdown, screenshots). Crawl extracts structured JSON data without creating local file copies. Use download for offline reading, crawl for data processing.

faq.question: Do I need a Firecrawl API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, the firecrawl CLI requires an API key. Set it via environment variable FIRECRAWL_API_KEY or configure through the CLI setup process.

faq.question: Can I download pages behind login or authentication?
faq.answer: The basic download command cannot authenticate. For authenticated sites, you would need to configure Firecrawl's authentication options or use alternative approaches like browser automation.

faq.question: How do I resume an interrupted download?
faq.answer: Downloads do not automatically resume. Re-run the same command - existing files will be overwritten. Use --limit and path filters to target specific sections if partial completion occurred.

faq.question: What formats are available for download?
faq.answer: Supported formats include markdown, HTML, links (extracted URLs), and screenshots (PNG). Use --format to specify multiple formats - each page gets separate files per format.

faq.question: How can I exclude certain pages from downloading?
faq.answer: Use --exclude-paths with comma-separated path patterns. For example: --exclude-paths '/blog,/changelog,/de,/fr' excludes blog, changelog, and language subdirectories.
